content:

Here we highlight a subset of our preferred stocks from the screener.

Hesai Group

Overview: Hesai Group develops, manufactures, and sells three-dimensional LiDAR solutions across Mainland China, Europe, North America, and internationally with a market cap of $3.56 billion.

Operations: Hesai Group's revenue segments are not specified in the provided text.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 23.8%

Hesai Group, trading at US$22.66, is undervalued by over 20% based on its discounted cash flow value of US$29.74. Despite shareholder dilution last year, Hesai's earnings are projected to grow significantly at 27.1% annually, surpassing the US market growth rate. Recent agreements with Sharpa and Grab enhance its LiDAR product distribution and revenue potential. However, Hesai's return on equity is forecasted to remain low at 12.4% in three years.

The analysis detailed in our Hesai Group growth report hints at robust future financial performance. Get an in-depth perspective on Hesai Group's balance sheet by reading our health report here.HSAI Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Glacier Bancorp

Overview: Glacier Bancorp, Inc. is a bank holding company for Glacier Bank, offering commercial banking services to individuals and organizations across the United States, with a market cap of approximately $6.26 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its banking services segment, which generated $952.72 million.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 25.5%

Glacier Bancorp, trading at US$48.13, is undervalued by over 20% based on its discounted cash flow value of US$64.59. Earnings are projected to grow significantly at 31.7% annually, outpacing the broader US market growth rate of 15.7%. Despite an unstable dividend track record, the company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.33 per share and has consistently increased dividends over time. However, return on equity is expected to remain low at 10.3% in three years.

Tutor Perini

Overview: Tutor Perini Corporation is a construction company offering general contracting, construction management, and design-build services to private and public sectors in the U.S. and internationally, with a market cap of approximately $4.40 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue segments are comprised of Civil (Including Management Services) at $3.06 billion, Building (Including Management Services) at $1.96 billion, and Specialty Contractors at $844 million.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 49.1%

Tutor Perini is trading at US$83.35, significantly below its estimated cash flow value of US$163.84, indicating substantial undervaluation. The company's earnings are expected to grow robustly at 54.6% annually, surpassing the US market's growth rate of 15.7%. However, high debt levels and unsustainable dividend coverage by earnings are potential concerns for investors.

title: Ferroglobe warns it may shut South Africa operations due to soaring electricity costs
content:
[Steel mill factory - molten metal in vat]
alexey_ds/E+ via Getty Images

Ferroglobe (GSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSM]) finished down 3.2% on Monday after warning [https://www.miningweekly.com/article/ferroglobe-may-halt-operations-if-not-afforded-reduced-electricity-tariffs-2026-03-30] it may be forced to shut down all its South Africa operations within days, citing a prolonged and unprecedented escalation in electricity prices.

The silicon metal and ferroalloy producer said it would have no alternative but to halt production across its facilities unless reduced electricity tariffs are approved before April 1, with dismissals of all remaining staff likely to take effect starting in June.

Ferroglobe (GSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSM]) said its electricity prices have increased by more than 900% since 2007 and now account for more than half of its total production cost, pushing production costs above selling prices and creating a severe disadvantage compared to international producers operating in regions where electricity prices are lower.

The company said a new tariff increase of nearly 9% by Eskom, South Africa's state-owned, primary electricity public utility, would deepen already severe financial losses.

Ferroglobe (GSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSM]) has built a significant industrial footprint over nearly three decades in South Africa, with assets including the Polokwane silicon metal smelter and the eMalahleni ferrosilicon smelter that have already suffered cutbacks in recent years.
